title: Criminal courts and criminal process
summary: 9084 — Magistrates', Crown Court, appeals, and criminal procedure from charge to sentence.
learningObjectives: Outline criminal court hierarchy and jurisdiction.
Explain classification of offences: summary, either-way, indictable.
Describe mode of trial and key trial stages.
Evaluate jury trial and magistrates' court role.

simpleExplanation.title: The criminal justice path
simpleExplanation.summary: Most cases start in Magistrates' Court; either-way offences may go to Crown Court for jury trial — appeals flow upward through the hierarchy.

sections.content: **Criminal courts** (1.2.3) — **offence classification** determines which court hears the case.
